fields.description:
## Dependencies (STACKED — review with --ref HEAD)
Builds on **ddbe** (identity entity with `mappings: [{provider, external_id}]`),
committed on this branch but not yet on origin/main. Review with `--ref HEAD`.

## Context
Resolve outbound Jira assignee/reporter by the provider's opaque **accountId** —
looked up through the identity entity's `{provider, external_id}` mappings behind a
provider seam — instead of by display-name/email string matching, with graceful
degradation. Mappings are keyed on the provider's stable opaque id, NEVER on email.

## Why
Jira's `/user/assignable/search` does fuzzy matching and can mis-assign or fail when
email is hidden. An identity carrying `{provider:"jira", external_id:<accountId>}`
lets the reconciler set the assignee by exact accountId and degrade to unassigned /
project-default rather than hard-failing.

## What
### 1. Provider-neutral resolution seam (`src/rebar/_commands/identity.py`)
- `resolve_mapping(provider, external_id, *, repo_root=None) -> str | None` — the id
  of the identity whose `mappings` contains `{"provider":provider,
  "external_id":external_id}` (exact match on the opaque id), else `None`. (AC1 seam;
  also consumed inbound by 2f13.) Never raises.
- `jira_account_id(local_assignee, *, repo_root=None) -> str | None` — resolve a LOCAL
  assignee string to a Jira accountId: find an identity whose ticket id equals
  `local_assignee` OR whose `email` equals it (case-insensitive); return that
  identity's `{provider:"jira"}` mapping `external_id`, else `None`. Never raises.

### 2. Assignee by accountId at the single Jira chokepoint (`acli.py`)
`AcliClient.validate_assignee_exists(assignee, issue_key=...)` is the ONE place a
local assignee becomes a Jira accountId, and BOTH the outbound differ
(`_assignee_resolver`, line ~491) AND `AcliClient.update_issue` (line ~354) and
`create_issue` (~301) call it — so making it identity-mapping-aware fixes the whole
path with NO sentinel, NO mutation/dataclass change, NO double-resolution:
- It first tries `identity.jira_account_id(assignee,
  repo_root=rebar.config.repo_root())` — using the AMBIENT reconcile repo, so NO
  `repo_root` needs threading through `AcliClient.__init__` or the call sites. If it
  returns an accountId, RETURN that accountId immediately (skip
  `/user/assignable/search`). Only on no mapping does it fall back to the existing
  assignable-search exact-match logic.
- Degradation UNCHANGED: an assignee that maps to neither an identity mapping nor an
  exact search match raises `AssigneeNotFoundError` → the existing soft-fail →
  unassigned/project-default. 264f never hard-fails a sync on an unresolvable assignee.

### 3. `/user/search` bootstrap (best-effort, TRANSIENT)
When an identity has an `email` but no `{provider:"jira"}` accountId mapping, a
best-effort `/rest/api/3/user/search?query=<email>` lookup (in `acli_rest.py`, used
inside `validate_assignee_exists`'s fallback) MAY resolve the accountId for the
CURRENT run only — it is NOT written back to the identity `mappings` (persistence is
out of scope). The v3 `/user/search` returns a JSON LIST of user objects each with an
`accountId` and `emailAddress`; the bootstrap extracts the `accountId` of the entry
whose `emailAddress` matches the identity's email EXACTLY (case-insensitive). Because
Jira's `/user/search` does multi-field substring matching, ZERO or TWO-OR-MORE exact
matches are treated as NO result (ambiguous → do not guess). A hidden email / no
result / an ambiguous multi-match all degrade to the existing search/unassigned path
WITHOUT failing.

### 4. Reporter by accountId — a DEDICATED REST sub-call (isolated failure)
Reporter is NOT bundled into the ACLI `client.update_issue(**fields)` scalar call
(that call runs one ACLI subprocess where a reporter-specific failure can't be
isolated, and ACLI's `workitem edit` may not expose `--reporter`). Instead, apply
reporter as its OWN sub-call in `update_one` — the same pattern labels/comments
already use (a separate dispatch branch, not a scalar field) — via the REST API:
`acli_rest._direct_rest_put(f"/rest/api/3/issue/{jira_key}", {"fields":
{"reporter": {"accountId": <resolved accountId>}}})`. Add a `set_reporter(jira_key,
account_id)` REST helper in `acli_rest.py`; `reporter` is emitted by the differ via
`outbound_fields.py` (diffed into `changed`), then in `update_one` a dedicated
`_update_one_apply_reporter` phase POPS `reporter` off the `fields` dict and routes it
to `set_reporter` — mirroring EXACTLY how `_update_one_apply_parent` pops `parent`
BEFORE `_update_one_filter_fields` runs the allowlist. Reporter is therefore NOT added to `_OUTBOUND_BATCH_ALLOWLIST` (it is handled
BEFORE `_update_one_filter_fields`, exactly like `parent`), so it can never flow into
`client.update_issue(**fields)`; the pop-before-filter phase is the sole route.
- Failure mode is HTTP-ONLY (no subprocess): a 403/400 from the reporter PUT means
  Modify-Reporter is not granted. Catch `urllib.error.HTTPError` (4xx) around ONLY
  this reporter sub-call, record a degradation via the existing soft-fail mechanism —
  `alert_store.append(...)` kind `outbound-reporter-not-permitted` (fields
  `jira_key`, `field="reporter"`, `reason`) — and return
  `HandlerResult(outcome, soft_failed=True)`. Because it is an isolated sub-call, the
  catch suppresses ONLY reporter failures; other fields in the same mutation are
  unaffected and the sync does NOT hard-fail.

## Scope
In: the `resolve_mapping`/`jira_account_id` seam, the identity-mapping fast-path in
`validate_assignee_exists`, the transient `/user/search` bootstrap, and reporter-by-
accountId-where-permitted with dual-mode degradation. Out: inbound ghost minting
(2f13), enforcement (3183), persisting bootstrapped accountIds. Tests use a fixture
identity + a stubbed client (no live Jira).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `resolve_mapping(provider, external_id)` returns the identity whose mappings
  contain that `{provider, external_id}` (keyed on the opaque id, not email) and
  `None` when absent; `jira_account_id` returns the identity's jira accountId for a
  matching local assignee and `None` otherwise (test asserts both).
- [ ] With a fixture identity mapping a local assignee to a stubbed Jira accountId,
  `validate_assignee_exists` returns that accountId WITHOUT calling the
  assignable-search path — asserted by a spy/stub whose `assignable_search`
  call-count is 0 (the measurable form of "no Jira search was needed").
- [ ] An assignee that resolves to no identity mapping and whose stubbed
  assignable-search misses degrades to unassigned/project-default WITHOUT raising or
  failing the sync (test).
- [ ] `/user/search` bootstrap: for an identity with an `email` but no
  `{provider:"jira"}` mapping, a stubbed `/user/search` returning an accountId lets
  `validate_assignee_exists` resolve the assignee to that accountId (transiently,
  not persisted); a stubbed miss degrades to the search/unassigned path without
  failing (test both).
- [ ] Reporter is applied via the dedicated `set_reporter` REST sub-call with the
  resolved accountId when the stub succeeds; when the stubbed reporter PUT raises an
  HTTP 4xx (Modify-Reporter not granted), the handler records an `alert_store` entry
  of kind `outbound-reporter-not-permitted` and returns `HandlerResult(soft_failed=
  True)` WITHOUT failing the sync or affecting other fields (test asserts the alert
  entry, that other fields still applied, and overall success).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (resolve_mapping + jira_account_id seam)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py` (validate_assignee_exists: identity mapping first via config.repo_root())
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_rest.py` (transient /user/search bootstrap + set_reporter REST helper)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py` (reporter field mapping)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py` (update_one: _update_one_apply_reporter pops reporter before the filter and routes it to set_reporter — mirrors _update_one_apply_parent)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_handlers.py` (reporter apply + dual-mode alert_store degradation)
